entry.title: Custom table data Migration from SAP ECC to S4Hana using with / without BODS
entry.content:
Hello All,

There is always bunch of discussions, approaches on custom table migrations from SAP ECC to S/4HANA.

I summarized the methods that been utilized in my experience.

Here's a detailed step-by-step approach for migrating custom tables from SAP ECC to S/4HANA using SAP BODS, including load methods and best practices:

📌 Objective:

To migrate custom Z-tables from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA using SAP BODS (BusinessObjects Data Services), ensuring data accuracy, auditability, and system integrity.

🔁 Phases of Custom Table Migration

✅ 1. Pre-requisites

Task

Details

📄 Identify Tables

List all custom tables (Z* tables) to be migrated

🔐 Access Setup

Ensure BODS has access to ECC and S/4HANA systems (create datastores)

📂 Data Volume

Estimate the number of records to handle capacity planning

🛠️ Coordination

Collaborate with ABAP team for BAPI/IDoc if needed

🧾 Version Check

Ensure compatibility of ECC extractors and S/4 targets

🔍 2. Source ECC Setup (BODS Extraction)

Step

Method

🟢 ECC Datastore in BODS

Create Application server-based datastore

📤 Extract Table

Use Table Reader transform to extract data from custom Z-table (e.g., ZCUSTOM_DATA)

📐 Data Cleansing

Apply transformation using Query transform, remove blanks, trim strings, convert formats

📋 Delta Logic (optional)

Filter with date fields or change documents for incremental loads

🧭 3. Target S/4HANA Load Method (Options)

Load Method

Description

Usage

Direct DB Insert

Insert into S/4 custom Z-table if direct DB access is allowed

Least preferred (integrity risk)

BAPI / RFC Call

Use custom BAPI or FM created by ABAP team to handle inserts/updates

Recommended

IDoc

Use custom IDoc (ZIDOC) type to post data into S/4

Reliable for large volumes

Intermediate Staging

Load into staging DB (e.g., HANA) and use SLT or ABAP program to post to S/4

When performance is a concern or no interface is available

🔧 Work closely with the ABAP team to create the required Z_BAPI_LOAD_CUSTOM or IDoc type (if not existing).

🔄 4. BODS Job Design

  • Workflow:
    • Source Table Read → Transformation → Target Load
  • Job Elements:
    • Batch Job with Error handling
    • Validation logic (null checks, date formats, etc.)
    • Use Case Transform or Validation transform if needed
  • Use Auditing and logging to record success/failure count

🧪 5. Testing & Validation

Type

Action

✅ Unit Test

Load limited records first

📊 Reconciliation

Match record counts, key fields between ECC and S/4

🔍 Functional Validation

Ensure data is correctly reflected in S/4 via GUI

🔄 Re-run Capability

Add timestamp or keys for delta re-run or rollback options

📦 6. Deployment & Post-Migration

  • Document the job flows and configurations
  • Schedule BODS jobs (via Management Console or third-party scheduler)
  • Monitor first few loads manually
  • Archive logs and reconciliation reports

🧰 Example: ZSALES_MIGR Table Migration

Source

ECC → ZSALES_MIGR

BODS reads data

from ECC → ZSALES_MIGR

Transforms data

cleans up codes, formats, adds audit info

Loads to S/4HANA

via Z_BAPI_LOAD_SALES_MIGR BAPI

Post-checks

Validate in S/4 GUI with TCode SE16N

📌 Final Tips

  • 🚫 Avoid direct DB inserts to S/4 unless approved
  • 🔁 If data is large, consider batch-wise chunking
  • 📤 Maintain logs for each load (successful + failed)
  • 💬 Always align with functionals to confirm logic and structure

Happy Learning. please share your comments and feedback.

Thanks,

Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Unable to post asset retirement with customer – system requires a cost object for revenue GL account
entry.content:
We are currently implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, focusing solely on the Finance (FI) module. As part of our asset accounting process, we are trying to post a fixed asset retirement integrated with a customer using the standard “Asset Retirement with Customer (Integrated)” app. During this process, we select the relevant customer and fixed asset, and assign a revenue G/L account for the transaction. Additionally, we enter the profit center as required by the client for reporting purposes.

However, upon simulation or posting, the system throws error KI248 stating:
"Account xxxxxxxx requires a true account assignment relevant to cost accounting."
The error indicates that the profit center assignment is considered only statistical and therefore not sufficient for revenue postings to this G/L account, which is defined as a Primary Cost or Revenue account with Cost Element Category 11 (Revenue).

Our client is not implementing or Profitability Analysis (CO-PA), and we do not have any real CO objects such as internal orders, WBS elements, or sales orders configured or available in scope. The requirement is simple: to record revenue against profit centers so financial statements can be generated by profit center dimension. We do not want to (and should not need to) maintain real CO objects, as they are not part of the project scope.

Despite entering a profit center, the system requires a true cost accounting object due to the nature of the cost element category. Since we are not implementing CO-PA, this requirement is currently blocking a valid business process in SAP Public Cloud. The error seems to enforce controlling behavior, even when controlling is not part of the activated scope in the cloud tenant.

Therefore, we request SAP Support to advise on how this can be resolved. Ideally, we would like to continue using a revenue G/L account (to reflect revenue properly in the P&L) and post to it with only a profit center, without the need for any additional CO-relevant object. If there is a configuration workaround, scope item adjustment, or recommendation for using a different G/L setup for such scenarios in SAP Public Cloud, please advise accordingly.

entry.title: How to have measures in between dimensions in SAC Table widget?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a project where I need to customize the layout of a table in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Specifically, I am required to position a measure between two dimensions in the table widget.

For instance, the default setup is as follows: Company Code, G/L Account, and Amount in TC. However, I need to rearrange it to: Company Code, Amount in TC, and G/L Account.

I understand that using a Measure-Based Dimension in a Calculated Dimension can achieve this.

 However, my challenge lies in the fact that my SAC table has numerous columns, and I'm unsure how to decide which columns should be included in the Dimension Context. If I add all the columns, it results in ‘null’ appearing in the calculated measure-based dimension.

Could you please guide me on how to efficiently determine which columns should be part of the Dimension Context, or if there's an alternative method to achieve the desired column arrangement without encountering null values?

entry.content:
How to view historical scrap records in the system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Scrap Document Reports
entry.content:
For inventory related scrap for the product in any logistics area is related to change in inventory, so the system generates a confirmation ID which updates finance as well for every scrap. These scrap records can be checked via the confirmation journal report with inventory change having respective confirmation ID.

We have reports also for inventory scrap against production orders and production-related scrap can be checked via Yield and Scrap report. (Report ID:SCMYASU01_Q0001). It provides the quantities of yield and scrap recorded for a production order.

entry.title: Re: S4 - Migration - Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello Aakash,

The tags have been adjusted accordingly to attract more attentions from my colleagues.

If you are talking about the Readiness Check report mentioned in Note 2913617 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA, it supports the Custom Code Analysis via ABAP Test Cockpit before migrating ERP to S/4HANA.

The ABAP test cockpit is an alternative custom code analysis tool supported by SAP Readiness Check. The ABAP test cockpit will perform a more in-depth analysis in comparison to the Custom Code Analyzer. However, it must be highlighted that there are additional steps required to enable this analysis. The process for establishing an ABAP test cockpit system for performing this analysis is documented here.

SAP Note 2781766 must be implemented on the system where the ABAP test cockpit is installed and enabled. If that system is >= SAP_BASIS 7.53 SP3 or >= SAP_BASIS 7.52 SP5, SAP Note 2781766 is not required, as the functionality is already available in those releases.

To create the ZIP archive in the ABAP test cockpit for upload to SAP Readiness Check, select the context menu option Export to -> File for SAP Readiness Check in the ABAP test cockpit result browser.

Similar to the Customer Code Analyzer, the ABAP test cockpit analysis is conducted outside of the program RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA and is later uploaded to the analysis created on the landing page of SAP Readiness Check.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: While performing the intra-STO process
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While performing the intra-STO process, I created a delivery using VL10B. However, when attempting to post the goods issue from the delivery, I encountered the following error:

 

Only  0 serial numbers entered instead of 1,000

Message no. IO304

Diagnosis

There is a serial number obligation, so the number of serial numbers must equal the number of serial numbers in the material document.

You can post the operation only if you entered the correct number of serial numbers previously.

System Response

Depending on the context in which the error arises, the system continues 

processing, or the required function cannot be performed.

Procedure

You have the following options, for example:
 

  • Check that the serial numbers are entered fully.
  • If necessary, display an error log.
  • If necessary, contact your system administrator.

 

Could you please assist in resolving this issue?
SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Calling OData services in SAP Digital Mnaufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Harsh-ctrl, i know that has been a while but...

 

1) i dont know if you are using Cloud Connector or not, but i'm assuming YES

2) you should use only http://{host}:{port}  on VIRTUAL HOST on servers

3) on your custom service registry you should attach your WebServer (as you did) but on the url/path you should not attach the entire url, but just the path that arrives by removing the web servers path, like this:

Path WebServer host/virtual url:  https://xxxx.yyyyy.com:443  (the same configured inside Cloud Connector)

 
Path Service Registry: /sap/opu/odata/SAP/XXXXXXXXXXXX/A_bbbbbbb

entry.title: Re: Stock data of serial number XXXXXXX not suitable for...
entry.content:
There is a KBA on this message: 3438882 - Error IO231 - Stock data of serial number not suitable for movement - during goods movement - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA 

  • Read the message long text for inconsistent field name, value in Serial Number (IQ03), value in mvt (MIGO)
  • In Support Content, look at Inconsistency in Material Management - Inventory Management if correction is not obvious.

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract - Distribution process
entry.content:
Dear Victor,

Thank you very much for your contribution, and apologies for the late reply — we took some time to evaluate how to apply your suggestions.

To reply to your questions:

  • reason for this approach? M: it is based on the business requirement to do not post the settlement if open accruals are not sufficient. Therefore, the process would be: 1. to adjust the accruals; 2. to post then the settlement. Purpose of the custom routine is to get this two steps in one, without any manual input.
  • Would it not be more effective to apply the logic of reversing open accruals only up to the net settlement amount? M: if we apply that new setup, how then they can get "missing Accruals" automatically posted, if any?

    Therefore: it is not clear to me how the solution you're suggesting should cover that requirement.

    Moreover, as additional scenario which is not covered at all: since our current Distribution Category is = 3 (std - Based on Accruals), how a manual settlement can be posted if there are no Accruals, given SD source volumes are totally absent due to a small lag between Accruals execution vs Payment execution? Should we enhance the Distribution Category applying a kind of "fall-back" logic, which also looks at a distribution based on "Settlement Documents" when there are no Accruals? Are there other std. ways to handle this scenario?

    Thanks again

    Michele

entry.title: Re: Integration SAP Public Cloud and SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hello,

To address the integration issue regarding the email address not being synchronized between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP Sales Cloud V2, you can take the following steps:

Steps to Resolve Email Synchronization Issue

  1. Check Master Data Configuration: Ensure that the email address field is correctly configured in both systems. In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, use the ‘Manage Customer Master Data’ app to confirm the email address is maintained for the contact person.

  2. Integration Settings: Verify the configuration settings for integration between SAP Public Cloud and Sales Cloud V2. Ensure that all relevant fields, including email address, are set to synchronize.

  3. Custom Integration Content: If there are custom fields or logic involved, check that these have been correctly implemented and mapped in your integration flows.

  4. Review SAP CPI Configuration: Utilize SAP CPI (SAP Cloud Platform Integration) to facilitate seamless communication between the two systems. Ensure OAuth authentication is properly set up from CPI to Sales Cloud V2 and from Sales Cloud to CPI.

  5. Debug and Log Analysis: Use debugging tools or logs to trace the flow of data. Identify any errors or points where the email data is not transferring correctly.

Resources

  • To maintain email addresses correctly in the SAP Public Cloud:

    • Manage Customer Master Data App 3090881 - Contact person’s email address is not copied over from customer master data - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides guidance on maintaining correct email addresses in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
  • For integration and synchronization:
  • Refer to SAP Integration Suite documentation SAP and SalesForce Custom Integration flows - SAP Community for integration flows between SAP S/4HANA and external systems like Sales Cloud.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Data Doesn’t Drive the Business - Trust Does. SAP Business Data Cloud Delivers.
entry.content:
@anthonywojcik honestly, I don't like your Blog!

Why? Because you are 30.000 feet above ground with it 🙄

Of course you are right, in an ideal world, if a company is fine with SAP standard, keeps the core clean and avoids any process or data changes outside of the predefined world. Then the Insight Apps will work perfectly.

But you ignore some vital facts - the real world! Companies are like they are: They want and have their own fields, their own processes and their own master data everywhere! This is the truth! And getting this into your ideal world scenario takes months or years of efforts. You know it, if you have S4 transition experience.

And one more thing: If you are an "architect, a data engineer" or someone developing or administrating developments in Datasphere - like me - then you know it is not the truth what you describe in a real world scenario. If you have several systems, then you can have different master data and then you have to harmonize it anyway.

You mix up different things in your blog and just describe the fancy marketing terms.

Your blog is not helpful at all, because it completely neglects the way to achieve everything you describe.

Let's face it: Majority of BW Customers will have the same efforts in PCE & BCD as if they would start all over again in Datasphere from the beginning ... because the end in 2030 is unavoidable in both cases, in PCE and on Prem.

The reality about trust is that SAP lost some trust... in BW Customers who have initially been told to leave their BW behind and start newly in SAP Datasphere. Then they had to recognize that they could have taken part of its over in the BW Bridge, one year later. And finally in 2025 those who started with the one or the other way are probably mad now, as they could have taken over their complete BW system into the private Cloud in BDC.

I could continue with even more topics, but I will stop here... I think you got the point?!

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite_Service Contract screen IF erro...
entry.content:
Hello,

To implement a scenario where pressing the ‘Save’ button Use SAP BTP Integration Suite to Integrate S4/HANA… on an S/4HANA Cloud Service Contract initiates integration with SAP BTP Integration Suite, the following steps can be taken:

  1. Trigger Integration Flow: You can configure the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to trigger an integration flow upon saving the Service Contract. This is typically done using communication arrangements that link specific actions in S/4HANA with integration flows on the SAP BTP Integration Suite.

  2. Handling Errors: To display error messages on the Service Contract screen when an error occurs during integration, the integration flow can be designed to send back response messages. Changes can be made in the integration flow to capture errors and relay them back to the S/4HANA Cloud system.

  3. Display Error Messages: SAP S/4HANA can be configured to listen for these error responses. Custom logic can be implemented in the UI—possibly extending the Service Contract application—to check for these responses and display appropriate error messages.

  4. Implement BTP Error Handling: On the BTP Integration Suite side, ensure that the integration flow has proper error handling in place. Use features such as error end events, throw alerts or messages, or write error logs that can be sent back to the originating system.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: A/P Invoice direct posting against more than one Cond...
entry.content:
Dear Victor,

many thanks for your input. Nevertheless, what you're proposing would be applicable for Manual Settlement process triggered by an external system (e.g TPM).

Even if the API you've suggested can cover the requirement from a technical perspective, the business requirement to be covered is: to post one A/P Invoice (e.g reference ID 1234) which than is split between:

  • Contract A - Manual Settlement 1111
  • Contract B - Manual Settlement 2222 Contract
  • C - Manual Settlement 3333

Although, from a legal standpoint, splitting an A/P Invoice ID (1234) vs Multiple Settlement Documents is not admitted. Therefore, a design solution that could be applied would be "2-Steps Manual Settlement" (with Collection). Given that, the process flow will be:

  1. To receive External A/P Invoice 1234
  2. To create Manual Settlement documents 1111 / 2222 / 3333
  3. To create Collective Settlement document, 9999 via WZRC t-code, which cumulate Settl. Documents. 1111 / 2222 / 3333 at collective settlement doc. item level

In this way, the legal requirement is covered: relationship between A/P Invoice & Settlement Document is always 1:1, as it is linked to the Collective Settlement document 9999.

I'm not sure if what I've reported is in line with your experience, but so far, we're facing a new challange regarding WZRC usage: since we would schedule an automatic job to execute it, how can we guarantee Settlement Documents are "cumulated" based on the proper A/P reference ID? In our basic example: the program should "understand" & collect Manual Settlement documents 1111 / 2222 / 3333 based on the Reference ID 1234 (which is the original A/P Invoice ID, that would be then tracked into each settlement document, as External Invoice ID).

Thank you in advance

Michele

entry.title: Re: On-premise SNC SAPCryptolib with publicly trusted CA...
entry.content:
This is not an answer to my question but rather a comment and suggestion for SAP.

You have a nice lean product which supports publicly trusted user certificates signed from SAP Cloud CA and well integrated into SAP IAS so we can easily use an existing corporate IdP such as Azure AD with MFA. BUT, now it's expected new customers must find some way to use the solution user pattern in a private PKI or self-signed SNC PSE so communications to systems can be trusted? This doesn't make sense and is an oversight in my opinion. Worst, this is poorly documented.

Suggestion: Create a feature or function in the existing cloud application similar to SAP Passport where a customer can simply download a trusted CA signed certificate chain suitable to be used for on-premise systems in the SNC PSE. The certificate will be generated based on existing customer user patterns and then imported in STRUST or via sapgenpse. This would complete an out-of-box solution.

entry.title: Configuring SAP S/4HANA for Diverse Withholding Tax Scenarios
entry.content:
Withholding Tax (WHT), also known as Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) in some countries like India, is a crucial aspect of tax compliance. Businesses need to accurately deduct and remit taxes on payments made to vendors and other parties. SAP S/4HANA offers robust functionalities to configure and manage various withholding tax scenarios, ensuring adherence to legal requirements and streamlining tax processes.

In some jurisdictions, the obligation to deduct withholding tax arises at the earliest of two events:

  • Upon Credit: When the amount is credited to the partner’s account, encompassing capital accounts.
  • Upon Payment: When the payment is actually made to the partner, regardless of the method (cash, cheque, draft, or other means).

This blog post delves into different withholding tax scenarios, with a particular focus on the scenario where deduction occurs at the earlier of credit or payment, detailing the necessary customization steps for configuring these scenarios in SAP S/4HANA.

1. Formula Based withholding tax Calculation

In scenarios where withholding tax rates vary based on progressive slabs, SAP S/4HANA offers the formula-based withholding tax calculation feature.

Scenario: Calculate withholding tax based on the following annual income slabs:

  • Up to ₹ 3,000: 15%
  • ₹ 3,001 to ₹ 10,000: 22%
  • Above ₹ 10,001: 34%

Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA:

A. Define withholding tax Code:

  • Navigate to IMG -> Extended withholding tax -> Calculation -> withholding tax Codes -> Define withholding tax Codes (or use transaction SPRO).
  • Create a new withholding tax code.
  • Crucially, activate the "W.tax form" flag (Technical field V_T059Z-XQFOR). This indicates that the calculation will be formula-based.

B. Define Formulas for Calculating withholding tax:

  • Go to IMG -> Extended withholding tax -> Calculation -> Define Formulas for Calculating withholding tax.
  • Create a new formula.
  • Enter the Validity date and Currency of the Company Code.
  • Define the slabs by entering the "Amount from", "Amount to", and the corresponding "Percentage". For the given scenario, you would define three steps in the formula. You can also define Reductions (optional) if applicable.

Amount from: 0, Amount to: 3000, Percentage: 15.

Amount from: 3001, Amount to: 10000, Percentage: 22

Amount from: 10001, (leave "Amount to" blank for infinity), Percentage: 34

System Behavior: When an invoice with this withholding tax code is processed:

  • The system will apply the defined formula to the withholding tax base amount.
  • It will calculate the withholding tax based on the progressive slabs defined in the formula.

 Example Calculation (Base Amount ₹ 12,000):

Using the defined formula, the calculation would be: (₹ 3,000 * 15%) + ((₹ 10,000 - ₹ 3,000) * 22%) + ((₹ 12,000 - ₹ 10,000) * 34%) = ₹ 450 + ₹ 1540 + ₹ 680 = ₹ 2670

 

2. TAN Based Exemption Limit Check (India Localization)

In India, businesses with a Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number (TAN) can obtain exemption certificates for certain vendors, allowing for withholding tax deduction only on the amount exceeding a specified limit. This exemption is often managed at the Permanent Account Number (PAN) level of the seller.

Scenario: withholding tax Section 194Q (withholding tax on purchase of goods) mandates withholding tax at 0.1% on the amount exceeding ₹ 50 lakh in a financial year from a seller if the total purchases from that seller exceed ₹ 50 lakh. This check needs to consider cumulative purchases across all vendors associated with the same seller PAN.

Configuration Steps in SAP ERP

A. Withholding tax Type for Invoice: Do not activate the standard accumulation functionality. Set the "Accumulation" to "No Accumulation".

B. Withholding tax Code for Invoice: Ensure that the "Calculation of withholding tax according to formula" checkbox is unchecked.

C. Mapping of Invoice and Payment WHT Types and Codes:

Map the Invoice time withholding tax type and code with the corresponding Payment time withholding tax type and code. This ensures consistency in exemption limit checks across both stages.

 

 

D. Maintain PAN in Vendor Master:

In the General Data view of the vendor master, under the "Vendor: Country-Spec. Enh." tab, maintain the PAN of the vendor.

E. Maintain TAN Exemption Data in Vendor Master (Company Code View):

Navigate to the Company Code view of the vendor master, under the "Vendor: Country-Spec. Enh." -> "India: TAN-Based Exemption" tab. Enter the following details:

  • Section code/ section code group (e.g., 194Q)
  • Certificate number issued by the tax authorities.
  • Exemption rate (the percentage of withholding tax exempted).
  • Exemption from and exemption to date (validity period of the certificate).
  • Reason for exemption.
  • Withholding tax code and tax type (for both invoice and payment).
  • Exemption threshold (e.g., 5000000 for ₹ 50 lakh). Ensure the same threshold is maintained for both invoice and payment types mapped to the same certificate number, even for duplicate vendors with the same certificate.
  • Currency.

System Behavior:

  • The system tracks the cumulative amount paid/credited across all vendors linked to the same PAN.
  • Once this cumulative amount exceeds the defined exemption threshold, withholding tax will be deducted on the excess amount for subsequent invoices or payments, considering the defined exemption rate.

 

3. Withholding Tax Grossing Up

Grossing up occurs when the payer (buyer) agrees to pay the seller (payee) a specific net amount and also covers the withholding tax liability on behalf of the seller. Essentially, the payer "grosses up" the payment so that after deducting the withholding tax, the seller receives the agreed-upon net amount.

In some countries, like Thailand, the concept of withholding tax grossing up is used. Here, the payer bears the withholding tax on behalf of the payee, resulting in the payee receiving the full invoice amount.

Scenario: A business needs to pay a vendor THB 1,000, and also pay the 5% withholding tax on the vendor's behalf.

Reason: This is often used in situations like:

  • Executive compensation or bonuses where the company wants the employee to receive a specific net amount.
  • Cross-border payments where the payer wants to ensure the foreign recipient receives the full intended amount without the burden of local withholding tax.

Accounting Impact: The payer accounts for the expense at a grossed-up value (net amount + withheld tax). The payment to the vendor is the agreed-upon net amount. The withheld tax is paid separately by the payer to the tax authorities, but it's treated as an additional cost/expense for the payer, not a deduction from the vendor's payment.

Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA:

A. Define withholding tax Code:

Navigate to IMG -> Extended withholding tax -> Calculation -> withholding tax Codes -> Define withholding tax Codes.

For the relevant withholding tax code, maintain the "Posting indicator" value as "2". This setting signifies that the tax amount should be posted to a separate expense account instead of reducing the vendor payment.

B. Define Accounts for withholding tax for "Gross.Up" Offsetting Entry:

Navigate to IMG -> Extended withholding tax -> Posting -> Define Accounts for withholding tax. 

For the relevant withholding tax type and code, configure the "Expense account for withholding tax". This is where the grossed-up tax amount will be debited.

 

System Behavior: When an invoice subject to gross-up is posted:

  • The system will calculate the withholding tax amount.
  • The accounting entry will reflect the following (as per the example):
    • Debit: Business partner (full invoice amount - ₹ 1,000)
    • Debit: Withholding tax expense (grossed-up tax amount - ₹ 52.63)
    • Credit: Bank (total payment - ₹ 1,000)
    • Credit: Withholding tax payable (tax liability - ₹ 52.63)

The system effectively pays the vendor the full amount while also accounting for the withholding tax expense separately. The grossed-up amount for tax calculation is derived such that applying the WHT rate to it yields the desired tax amount. In the example, if the invoice is ₹ 1,000 and the WHT rate is 5%, the grossed-up amount is ₹ 1,052.63 (₹ 1,000 / (1 - 0.05)), and 5% of ₹ 1,052.63 is approximately ₹ 52.63.

 

Understanding Accumulation/Threshold Limit Checks

A fundamental concept in withholding tax is the accumulation/threshold limit check. This mechanism determines when withholding tax becomes applicable based on the total amount paid or credited to a supplier within a specific period. SAP S/4HANA, similar to SAP ECC, traditionally performs this check at the vendor master level.

Let's explore different variations of this check:

4. Accumulation Limit Check at Monthly Level

In certain scenarios, like rent payments, withholding tax might be applicable only when the monthly payment exceeds a specific threshold.

Scenario: Deduct withholding tax on rent payments only when the monthly rent exceeds ₹ 50,000.

Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

A. Set Accumulation Type:

  • Navigate to the Define withholding tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key (e.g., IN for India).
  • Locate the withholding tax type relevant to your requirement (e.g.: Section 194I (Rent)).
  • Set the "Accumulation type" to "Per month". This instructs the system to track payments monthly for this WHT type.
  • Verify that the corresponding withholding tax code(s) for your withholding tax section (e.g. 194I) have the correct withholding tax rate defined.

B. Set Minimum Amount:

  • Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for withholding tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Find the withholding tax code associated with Section 194I.
  • Set the "Minimum base amount" to 50000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025 (assuming the new fiscal year starts then).

C. Vendor Master Assignment:

Ensure the relevant withholding tax code for 194I is assigned to the respective vendors in the Vendor Master data.

System Behaviour: When a rent invoice is entered for a vendor with this configuration:

  • The system will check the total rent paid to that vendor within the current month.
  • If the cumulative monthly rent exceeds ₹ 50,000, withholding tax will be calculated on the entire amount of the current invoice.

 

5. Accumulation Check at Transaction Level

In some cases, withholding tax applicability depends on the value of a single transaction, irrespective of the total amount paid to the vendor.

Scenario: Deduct withholding tax when a single transaction amount for lottery or game winnings is ₹ 10,000 or more (Section 194B).

Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

A. Set No Accumulation:

  • Go to the Define withholding tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Find the withholding tax type for Section 194B.
  • Set the "Accumulation" field to "No accumulation". This ensures each transaction is evaluated independently.

B. Set Minimum Amount:

  • Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for withholding tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Locate the withholding tax code for Section 194B.
  • Set the "Minimum base amount" to 10000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025.

C. Vendor Master Linkage: Ensure the appropriate withholding tax code for 194B is linked to the relevant vendors in the Vendor Master.

System Behavior:

When a payment is made for lottery or game winnings:

  • The system will evaluate the amount of that specific payment.
  • If the single transaction amount is ₹ 10,000 or more, withholding tax will be deducted.

 

6. Accumulation Limit Check at Financial Year Level

With evolving tax regulations, new sections might be introduced with accumulation based on the financial year.

Scenario: Implement withholding tax under the newly introduced Section 194T, applicable to payments (remuneration, interest, commission) made by a firm to its partners, where withholding tax at 10% is levied if the total payment exceeds ₹ 20,000 in a financial year (starting April).

Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

A. Define withholding tax Key:

  • Access the Define withholding tax Keys configuration activity (ID: 104229).
  • Create a new entry for 194T with a relevant description (e.g., "withholding tax on Partner Payments").

B. Define withholding tax Type:

  • Use the Define withholding tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Create a new withholding tax type for 194T.
  • Set the "Accumulation type" to "Per year starting with month: 4". (for Apr to Mar FY) This configures the system to track payments annually, starting from April.

C. Define withholding tax Code:

  • Create a new withholding tax code specifically for 194T.
  • Set the tax rate to 10%.
  • Assign the withholding tax key (194T) and the newly created withholding tax type to this code.

D. Set Minimum Amount:

  • Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for withholding tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Locate the withholding tax code for 194T.
  • Set the "Minimum base amount" to 20000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025.

E. Payment Posting WHT Type and Code (Optional): If required by your business processes, similarly create a corresponding withholding tax type and withholding tax code for payment postings related to Section 194T.

F. Vendor Master Assignment: In the Vendor Master for partners (treated as vendors), ensure the newly created withholding tax code(s) for 194T are added to their records.

System Behavior: When processing invoices or payments to partners:

  • The system will track the cumulative amount paid to that partner within the financial year (April to March).
  • Once the total exceeds ₹ 20,000, withholding tax at 10% will be deducted on the subsequent payments.

Conclusion

Configuring SAP S/4HANA for different withholding tax scenarios requires a thorough understanding of the specific legal requirements and careful implementation of the system's functionalities. By correctly setting up accumulation types, threshold limits, formula-based calculations, TAN-based exemptions, and gross-up mechanisms, businesses can ensure accurate withholding tax deductions, comply with regulations, and streamline their tax processes within the SAP environment. Remember to always test your configurations thoroughly in a non-production environment before deploying them in your live system.

Read more at the SAP Help Portal. Subscribe to this blog post to get informed about updates automatically.

entry.title: SAP ME 15.5 ClassCastException
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

After a success deployment , did you see the following error in the SAP ME NWA log ?

This error didn't appear in SAP ME version 15.4 , only in 15.5 version.

[EXCEPTION]
java.lang.ClassCastException: java.lang.reflect.WeakCache (loaded by <bootstrap>) cannot be cast to java.util.Map (loaded by <bootstrap>)
at com.sap.me.frame.AppStartupListener.clearProxyCache(AppStartupListener.java:93)
at com.sap.me.frame.AppStartupListener.contextDestroyed(AppStartupListener.java:63)
at com.sap.engine.services.servlets_jsp.server.application.WebEvents.contextDestroyed(WebEvents.java:169)

entry.title: Indian Budget 2025: Managing the Omission of TCS Section 206C(1H) in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The Budget 2025 has brought a significant relief by removing the TCS provision under Section 206C(1H) on the sale of goods, effective from April 1, 2025. Previously, sellers were required to collect TCS if the aggregate value of goods sold exceeded ₹ 50 lakhs, subject to other conditions. This often led to overlaps and compliance complexities with the TDS provision under Section 194Q, where buyers had to deduct TDS on similar transactions.

Section

Before 1st April 2025

From 1st April 2025

206C(1H) - Purchase of Goods

50,00,000

Nil (No TCS Applicable)

This blog post outlines the necessary steps within the FI and SD modules of SAP to ensure your system reflects this change and ceases TCS calculation for relevant transactions from the specified date.

 

Delimiting TCS Calculation in SAP FI Module

This change necessitates adjustments in your FI module of SAP S/4HANA (On-prem and Cloud) system to ensure compliance and avoid errors. Follow these recommendations:

A. Tax Codes with Only TCS 206C(1H):

  • Discontinue the use of any tax codes in your SAP system that exclusively contain the TCS rate under Section 206C(1H) starting from April 1, 2025.
  • Modify the description of these tax codes to clearly indicate "Not Applicable from April 1st, 2025" to prevent accidental selection by users.

B. Tax Codes with Combined GST and TCS 206C(1H):

  • If you have existing tax codes that combine GST rates with the TCS rate under Section 206C(1H), prioritize using separate tax codes containing only the applicable GST rates.
  • Create new tax codes specifically for GST rates if they don't already exist. This approach ensures clarity and simplifies future tax rate adjustments.
  • Similar to point 1, update the description of the combined GST and TCS tax codes to indicate "Not Applicable from April 1st, 2025".

C. Avoid Modifying Existing Tax Codes with TCS:

  • Refrain from directly modifying the TCS rates within existing tax codes that have been used in past financial postings. This can lead to inconsistencies when reversing documents posted before April 1, 2025.
  • During year-end closing activities in April or May, you might need to post documents with posting dates prior to April 1, 2025. The original tax codes with TCS will be required for these historical postings.
  • Altering previously used tax codes can compromise the integrity of your financial records.

 

Delimiting TCS Calculation in SAP SD Module

Here are the steps to delimit TCS calculation in your SAP SD system:

A. Modify the Validity End Date of TCS Condition Records:

The TCS calculation in SAP SD is typically managed through condition records maintained in pricing procedures. You need to identify and update the validity of these condition records to reflect the discontinuation of TCS collection from April 1, 2025.

  • Identify TCS Condition Names: The standard condition names for TCS might be JTC1 and JTCB. However, these might be customized in your system. To verify the correct condition names mapped to your pricing procedure(s), consult the table J_1IEXCDEFN.
  • Update Condition Records: Once you have identified the correct condition names, execute transaction VK12 (Change Condition). For each identified TCS condition record, modify the "Validity to" date to 31st March 2025.

Note: Ensure you perform this step for all relevant pricing procedures where TCS is currently configured.

B. Verify Copy Control Settings for Redetermination of Taxes:

When creating billing documents with reference to preceding documents (like deliveries or sales orders), the system's copy control settings determine how pricing conditions are handled. To ensure that the updated validity of the TCS condition records is considered, you need to verify that the pricing type is set to redetermine taxes.

  • Access Copy Control Transactions: Navigate to the appropriate copy control transaction based on the preceding document:
    • VTFL: For invoices created with reference to a delivery.
    • VTFA: For invoices created with reference to a sales order.
    • VTFF: For invoices created with reference to another billing document.
    • (Choose the relevant transaction code(s) like B, C, or G based on your specific business scenarios).
  • Check Pricing Type: Within the copy control settings for the relevant item category mapping, verify the "Pricing type" field. If it is set to "Fix values" or "Unchanged", the system will copy the JTC1 condition value from the source document without redetermining it. This could lead to unwanted TCS calculation even after March 31, 2025, if the source document was created before this date.
  • Adjust Pricing Type (If Necessary): If the pricing type is set to "Fix values" or "Unchanged", consider changing it to a pricing type that includes tax redetermination (e.g., 'B' - Carry out new pricing). Evaluate the impact of this change on other pricing elements before making the modification.

C. Manage Pricing Dates for Invoices in the Current Financial Year (April 2025 onwards):

For invoices that you wish to post in the current financial year (starting April 1, 2025) and onwards, you need to ensure that the pricing date passed to the billing document is on or after 01.04.2025. This will ensure that the system does not consider the (now expired) TCS condition records during pricing.

  • Impact of Goods Issue Date: Be aware that if the goods issue date (or service rendered date) in the preceding document was within the validity period of the TCS condition records (i.e., before or on 31st March 2025), this date might be proposed as the pricing date in the billing document, potentially leading to TCS calculation.
  • Modifying Pricing Date: In such scenarios, you will need to modify the pricing date in the billing document (transaction VF02) to a date on or after 01.04.2025 to avoid TCS calculation.
  • Pricing Date Control: The default determination of the pricing date in the billing document is controlled by the settings you have maintained for the pricing date field in transaction V/06 (Sales Document Item Category). Ensure your configuration aligns with your requirement to avoid unwanted TCS calculation. You might need to review and potentially adjust this configuration based on your business needs.

 

Additional Information

For users of the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, refer to SAP Note 3576728 for detailed steps and considerations related to this abolition. Similarly, customers utilizing S/4HANA On-premise environments should consult SAP Note 3574318 for the necessary adjustments within their systems to reflect the removal of this TCS requirement.

 

Conclusion

By carefully following these steps, you can effectively delimit the TCS calculation in your SAP FI and SD modules from April 1, 2025. Remember to thoroughly test these changes in a non-production environment before implementing them in your live system to ensure a smooth transition and accurate billing processes. Always consult with your SAP consultants for any complex configurations or specific business requirements.

entry.title: Re: Authorization for SAP Readiness Check
entry.content:
As guided by KBA 3455928 - Authorizations required for the Readiness Check application - SAP for Me, S-user IDs must have the authorizations below to view and maintain data in the SAP Readiness Check application in SAP for Me.

  • Display SAP Readiness Check Analysis (RC_ANA_DIS)
    Enables S-users to view all SAP Readiness Check analyses belonging to the customer number associated with their user ID.

  • Manage SAP Readiness Check Analysis (RC_ANA_MGT)
    Enables S-users to create, edit, delete, and share all SAP Readiness Check analyses belonging to their customer number.

Any super administrator listed in SAP for Me > My Important Contacts can grant the necessary authorizations to your S-user, or use the self-service authorization request according to KBA 2891528 to request them.

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 2
entry.content:
Hello @matthias_schmalz ,

Thank you for your response again, truly sorry that I think I misspoke and described my issue wrongly. 

What we are trying to achieve is to run 2 separate full-fledge BTP CAP UI5 applications in the same browser window so that to provide a seamless experience for the user, i.e., the user will open app1 first and app 1 will receive input from the user and then activate app 2 (the reuse app) in the same view/window via SAPUI5's Fragment and thus we need to pass the received input from app 1 to app 2 (reuse app), so the user can initiate the UI of app 2 (reuse app) with the correct parameters. 

An example code snippet below to show app 2 (the reuse app) in a UI5 fragment from app1:

The xml code from App1

<core:FragmentDefinition
	xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:core="sap.ui.core">
	<core:ComponentContainer usage="resuse" manifest="true" async="true" />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

The Controller code from App1

// some methods from the App1 controller
async openDialog(sName) {
        // to create the dialog lazily
        this.oDialog ??= await this.loadFragment({
          name: sName,
        });

        this.oDialog.open();
      }

// the press event handler of a button in app1 to open up the reuse app in a fragment
onOpenReuseApp() {
        this.openDialog('app1ui.view.app1Fragments.ReuseAppFragment');
      }

 

Thank you very much and again, sorry for the inconveniences caused.

entry.title: Re: High memory alert
entry.content:
Free Memory metric from Linux template is measuring available physical Memory :

This the amount of physical memory free on the host in gigabyte.

The metric returns data in GB.

However this metric is not capable of measuring in %, but you have metric Free Memory [%]

This the amount of physical memory free on the host in percentage of total memory available.

entry.title: Indian Budget 2025: Navigating TDS Changes in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The Indian Budget 2025 has brought forth significant amendments to the Tax Deduction at Source (TDS) provisions under the Income Tax Act, 1961. These changes aim to simplify tax compliance for both businesses and individuals by introducing higher threshold limits and streamlining overall taxation processes.

This article will delve into the some of the key changes in TDS and provide a practical guide on how to implement these updated requirements within your SAP S/4HANA system.

 

Enhanced Threshold Limits For TDS

A crucial aspect of the Budget 2025 is the increase in threshold limits for TDS applicability under certain sections. This means that TDS will only be triggered when the transaction value exceeds these new, higher limits. The revised thresholds, effective from April 1, 2025, are outlined below:

Section

Before 1st April 2025

From 1st April 2025

194B - Winnings from lottery, crossword puzzle Etc.

194BB - Winnings from horse race

Aggregate of amounts exceeding 10,000/- during the financial year

10,000/- in respect of a single transaction

194-I - Rent

2,40,000 (in a financial year)

50,000 per month

Let's explore how to configure SAP S/4HANA to align with these enhanced threshold limits for specific TDS sections:

For TDS Section 194I (Rent Payments)

To ensure TDS is deducted only when monthly rent payments exceed ₹ 50,000, follow these customization steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

Set Accumulation Type:

  1. Navigate to the Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  2. Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  3. Locate the withholding tax type for 194I.
  4. Set the "Accumulation type" to "Per month". This instructs the system to track payments on a monthly basis.
  5. Verify that the corresponding withholding tax code(s) for 194I have the correct TDS rate.

 

Set Minimum Amount:

  1. Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  2. Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  3. Find the withholding tax code for your 194I.
  4. Set the "Minimum base amount" to INR 50,000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025.

Ensure the relevant withholding tax code is assigned to the respective vendors in the Vendor Master. Now, upon entering a rent invoice, the SAP S/4HANA system will:

  • Check if the total rent paid to the vendor within that specific month has surpassed ₹ 50,000.
  • If the threshold is crossed, TDS will be calculated on the entire amount of that month's invoice.

 

For TDS Section 194B (Lottery/Game Winnings):

To deduct TDS when a single winning amount from lottery or games is ₹ 10,000 or more, implement the following configurations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

Set No Accumulation:

  1. Go to the Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  2. Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  3. Find the withholding tax type for 194B.
  4. Set the "Accumulation" to "No accumulation". This ensures that each transaction is evaluated independently against the threshold.

Set Minimum Amount:

  1. Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  2. Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  3. Locate the withholding tax code for your 194B.
  4. Set the "Minimum base amount" to 10,000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025.
  5. Ensure the appropriate withholding tax code is linked to the relevant vendors in the Vendor Master.

With this configuration, the system will:

  • Evaluate each payment for lottery or game winnings individually.
  • Deduct TDS if the amount of a single transaction is ₹ 10,000 or more.

  

TDS on Partner’s Remuneration Section-194T

A significant addition in the Budget 2025 is the introduction of Section 194T, which mandates TDS on certain payments made by a firm (partnership firm or LLP) to its partners. Previously, TDS was not applicable to payments like salary, bonus, commission, or interest to partners.

Key Provisions of Section 194T:

Applicability: Any firm responsible for paying salary, remuneration, commission, bonus, or interest to a partner.

TDS Rate: 10%.

TDS Threshold Limit: TDS is applicable only if the aggregate amount paid or credited to a partner during the financial year exceeds ₹ 20,000.

Timing of TDS Deduction: TDS must be deducted at the earlier of the following two events:

  • At the Time of Credit: When the sum is credited to the partner’s account, including capital accounts.
  • At the Time of Payment: When the payment is made to the partner, whether through cash, cheque, draft, or other modes.

Payment

TDS Rate

TDS Threshold Limit

Remuneration, Interest and Commission paid to partners

10%

Exceeds Rs. 20,000 in a financial year.

Steps to Configure 194T in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

A. Define Withholding Tax Key:

  • Access the Define Withholding Tax Keys configuration activity (ID: 104229).
  • Create a new entry for 194T with a relevant description.

B. Define Withholding Tax Type:

  • Use the Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting activity (ID: 104225).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Create a new withholding tax type for 194T.
  • Set the "Accumulation type" to "Per year starting with month: 4". This ensures annual tracking of payments from April to March.

 

C. Define Withholding Tax Code:

  • Create a new withholding tax code specifically for 194T.
  • Set the tax rate to 10%.
  • Assign the withholding tax key and type created in the previous steps.

D. Set Minimum Amount:

  • Open the Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Tax Codes activity (ID: 103915).
  • Choose Configure and select your country/region key.
  • Locate the withholding tax code for 194T.
  • Set the "Minimum base amount" to INR 20,000 and the "Valid from date" to 01.04.2025.

E. Similarly, create a corresponding withholding tax type and withholding tax code for payment postings, if required by your business processes.

F. In the Vendor Master (for partners treated as vendors), ensure the newly created withholding tax code(s) for 194T are added to the partner's record.

 

Exemption Limit vs. Accumulation/Threshold Limit in TDS (India)

In the context of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) in India, it's crucial to distinguish between how the "exemption limit" and the "accumulation/threshold limit" are applied. These determine when and how TDS is triggered.

Exemption Limit Check:

  • Concept: Under an exemption limit check, TDS is levied only on the amount that exceeds a specified limit. Any cumulative amount paid or credited to a supplier beyond this limit becomes subject to TDS deduction.
  • SAP Implementation (India Localization): In SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC, the exemption limit check, as described, operates specifically for TAN-based exemptions at the vendor-PAN level. This means:
    • The exemption limit is defined at the vendor master level under the "Country India" tab, based on the exemption certificate number.
    • The system tracks the cumulative amount paid/credited across all vendors associated with the same Permanent Account Number (PAN).
    • Once the cumulative amount across these vendors exceeds the specified exemption limit, TDS is deducted on the excess amount for subsequent invoices or payments.
  • Example (TDS Section 194Q): As highlighted, Section 194Q stipulates that TDS at 0.1% is applicable on the amount exceeding ₹50 lakh in a financial year from a seller from whom purchases worth more than ₹50 lakh have been made.
    • If a buyer with multiple vendors under the same seller PAN cumulatively purchases goods worth ₹55 lakh, TDS will be deducted on ₹5 lakh (₹55 lakh - ₹50 lakh) on the subsequent invoice or payment.

 

Accumulation/Threshold Limit Check:

  • Concept: With an accumulation/threshold limit check, TDS becomes applicable the moment the total amount paid or credited to a supplier in a financial year crosses a predefined threshold. Once this threshold is breached, TDS is deducted on the entire amount paid or credited to that specific supplier for the financial year.
  • SAP Implementation: At present in SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC, the accumulation/threshold limit check is performed at the individual vendor level, irrespective of how many vendor masters exist for the same PAN.
  • Example (TDS Section 194I): Consider the provided scenario for Section 194I with a monthly threshold of ₹50,000 for rent:
    • ABC Ltd. pays ₹75,000 monthly rent to Mr. Sharma (one vendor).
    • Since the monthly rent of ₹75,000 exceeds the ₹50,000 threshold, TDS is applicable on the entire ₹75,000 for that month.
    • The fact that ABC Ltd. might have other landlords (different vendors, potentially with the same PAN as Mr. Sharma for other properties) is irrelevant for this specific vendor and the ₹50,000 monthly threshold for rent under Section 194I. TDS will be calculated on the full rent paid to each landlord individually if their respective rent exceeds the threshold.

 

Additional Information

For further detailed guidance and specific configuration steps within SAP S/4HANA, especially considering whether you are using the cloud or on-premise version, SAP has provided several helpful resources.

Refer to the SAP Notes 3587527 and 3580598 for changes related to TDS sections 194I and 194B in Cloud and On-premise environments, respectively.

Specific configurations for the newly introduced Section 194T on payments by partnership firms to partners are detailed in the SAP Notes 3590606 and 3590334.

Additionally, comprehensive information on configuring TAN Based Exemption can be found in the SAP Help Portal.

For a holistic understanding of withholding tax configurations, both Withholding Tax Configurations - Cloud and Withholding Tax Configuration – On-prem pages on the SAP Help Portal offer valuable insights.

Leveraging these resources will ensure accurate and efficient implementation of the updated TDS regulations in your SAP S/4HANA system.

 

Conclusion

By carefully implementing these configuration steps and adhering to the recommendations, you can effectively align your SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC system with the updated TDS provisions of the Budget 2025, ensuring smooth tax compliance and accurate financial reporting. Remember to thoroughly test these changes in a non-production environment before implementing them in your live system to ensure a smooth transition and accurate financial and billing processes. Always consult with your SAP consultants for any complex configurations or specific business requirements.

entry.title: Re: Downpayment for Poland
entry.content:
Hi,

have you implemented the solution according to instructions in the SAP note? As I mentioned, the system does not require the use of the pricing procedure RVAB02 in the whole process, only in the downpayment invoice. If you implement the note step by step, the process will be as follows (from a pricing perspective):

  1. Sales order - your custom pricing procedure
  2. Downpayment invoice - pricing procedure RVAB02 (downpayment amount needs to be entered manually in the document).
  3. Final / partial invoice - your custom pricing procedure

Best regards

entry.title: The functionalities and components of SAP are influenced by Business User Management (CA-GTF-BUM)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Folks,
I’m seeking clarification regarding specific SAP configurations or components that necessitate the use of a Business User.

I’ve reviewed several SAP Notes, including:
2570961 – Simplification item S4TWL - Business User Management
2763781 – SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Use Sales Employee in SD without HR integration
3094750 – Business User Management - FAQ

However, these resources do not comprehensively list the full range of functionalities or modules that depend on the Business User Management.
From my current understanding and experience, I know that the Responsibility Management for Flexible Workflow and Situation Handling are two key functionalities that require users to be defined as Business Partners with the BUP003 role.

I’m interested to know if there are additional SAP functionalities or components, based on your implementation experience that also depend on this role. Specifically, I’m trying to assess pros and cons to use Business User (BP with role BUP003) for our scope or if we can continue using the classic SU01 user model for certain processes.

Any insights, especially those backed by implementation experience in SAP S/4HANA, would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance for your support and shared knowledge.

entry.title: Discover How SAP's IT Drives Transformation and Innovation
entry.content:
SAP is pulling back the curtain to provide customers and prospects an inside look to reveal how we bring our best to life. We don’t just sell software; we use it to drive our own success. Learn how SAP uses our own technology to find the better business within ourselves. Our technology. At work.

Register here: https://go4.events.sap.com/inside-sap-2025/en_us/home.html

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
It's also possible with:
1. BW Connection to SAP Datasphere
 2. Import Remote Table based on a BW InfoProvider
3. Choose "Disk" or "In Memory" for data storage (Remote Table)
3. Enable Real-time Data Replication. BW ODQ is automatically involved - push

works without BDC or the new DataGenerator(?)

entry.title: Re: Streamlining User Management: Integrating SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
Yeah I get that but in the way the setup is described there is no need for that and using LID over IAS saves a lot of nurses with certificates etc.

Also not really understanding yet why you are both reading and writing back and forth but haven't had time to deep dive yet

entry.title: Re: Can I have a scroll bar for sap.m.tree in Mobile devi...
entry.content:
Hello VaisakhSatheesan,

Mobile operating systems often hide scrollbars by default to provide a cleaner user interface, showing them only briefly during scrolling.

Regards,
Farooq

entry.title: Re: Advanced Bank Account Management (J77) - Create Bank...
entry.content: @Gabor5 Have you checked the configuration activity Manage Field Status Groups? The contact person fields may have been set to hidden.
